_comment_crash: T2.4 crash reporting (Sentry envelope). Opt-in, default OFF. crashReportingPrompted tracks whether the first-run consent modal has been answered — no report is ever sent until the user opts in (crashReportingEnabled=1 AND crashReportingPrompted=1). Reports carry only an allowlist of non-PII fields (release, environment, error type/message, code location) — never transcripts, audio, the connection key, the license, the username, or the machine name.
_comment_license: T2.3 trial/license fields below are DOCUMENTATION ONLY. The authoritative, DPAPI-encrypted state lives in %APPDATA%\QuickSay\license.dat (see lib/license.ahk) — these keys are NOT read from config.json, and the license JWT is NEVER stored here in plaintext.
_comment_telemetry: T2.7 opt-in telemetry (PostHog HTTP, no SDK). OFF by default — nothing is sent until the user opts in via Settings → Privacy. telemetryInstallId is a random UUID (CoCreateGuid), never derived from hardware, never correlatable to the license/trial identity. It is regenerated on each opt-out→opt-in transition so the timeline genuinely breaks. See Development/docs/telemetry-events.md for the full event list. Never sent: transcripts, audio, API keys, license JWT, email, username, machine name, MAC address, or full file paths.
